_meta.purpose: Dispatch map for the Discovery Tetrad backward-audit run. Each pair is one parallel-agent unit. Per the authors 2026-05-07: NO pre-extraction of goals. Each agent reads goal_source FIRST to populate goals + map to witness, then evaluates witness, then emits one schema-valid JSON per goal into <substrate>/outputs/. The output filename is `<goal.id>.json`.
_meta.schema_note: All file paths are RELATIVE to empirical_analysis/. supplementary_context entries are read by the agent for substrate-context but are not the primary goal/witness payload.

design_locks_2026_05_07.flt_goal_source: final.json (world model) + origin.json (typing plan) — agent reads these FIRST and does goal-to-witness mapping at runtime; witness = the .lean file
design_locks_2026_05_07.slt_goal_source: fallback rule (no blueprint file exists in the SLT repo) — same .lean file is goal_source AND witness
design_locks_2026_05_07.fp_goal_source: PDF (problem statement at top); witness = same PDF (proof body). NO step-level decomposition (the authors lock 2026-05-07: 'FP is 10 ONLY'). Existing GPT-as-judge yamls in fp/datasets/yaml/ are reserved for comparison appendix and are NOT fed to the agent for this run (avoids contamination).
design_locks_2026_05_07.asv1_goal_source: ideas.json (~51 entries per paper); witness = paper.pdf

pairs.extraction_policy: Read final.json (the FLT world model: paradigm_joints, structural_fractures, measurability_hierarchy, mathlib_bridges, resolved_sorrys, new_in_v2/v3/v3_2). Identify the named goals that map to this witness file using file_structure (L0-L7 + Bridge + PureMath layers list explicit file paths) and theorem-name inference. For each matched goal: populate the goal block from final.json's structure, then read the witness .lean file to evaluate the theorem statement and proof term. Emit one schema-valid JSON per matched goal, named `<goal.id>.json`, into flt/outputs/. Auxiliary lemmas in the witness file that are NOT named in final.json should NOT be emitted as separate goals.
